_comment: The two timelines: About-page history and Ordination-page movement milestones. Add an entry, pick a year, optionally attach an image and a link.

aboutHistory.title: Founded in Bodh Gaya, India
aboutHistory.text: The first Sakyadhita International Conference is held at the site of the Buddha's awakening. Founding members: Ayya Khema, Chatsumarn Kabilsingh, Karma Lekshe Tsomo.

aboutHistory.title: First Sakyadhita Newsletter
aboutHistory.text: Launch of the biannual newsletter, now in its 33rd volume. Distributed in print and PDF across 45 countries.

aboutHistory.title: Sarnath ordinations
aboutHistory.text: Sri Lankan women ordained in Sarnath, India, marking the restoration of the Theravada bhikkhuni order after a centuries-long interruption.

aboutHistory.title: Return to Vaishali
aboutHistory.text: 13th Conference held in Vaishali, India, where Mahapajapati Gotami became the first Buddhist nun. A symbolic homecoming.

aboutHistory.title: First virtual conference
aboutHistory.text: 17th Conference held online during the pandemic. Over 1,200 participants from 38 countries, in 7 working languages.

aboutHistory.title: 19th Conference, Sarawak
aboutHistory.text: "Navigating Change: Buddhist Women in Transition" in Kuching, East Malaysia. Hosted by the Buddhist Society of Sarawak.

ordinationMovement.title: The founding conversation
ordinationMovement.text: The first Sakyadhita conference in Bodh Gaya identifies women's ordination as the movement's signature issue.

ordinationMovement.title: The revival of bhikkhunī ordination in the Theravada tradition
ordinationMovement.text: Sri Lankan women ordained in Sarnath, India, marking the restoration of the Theravada bhikkhuni order.

ordinationMovement.title: International Full Ordination Ceremony in Bodhgaya, India
ordinationMovement.text: Bhiksunis Thubten Chodron and Karma Lekshe Tsomo were part of the bhikshuni sangha that gave dual ordination to new bhiksunis and bhikkhunis at Bodhgaya, India.

ordinationMovement.title: Hamburg Congress
ordinationMovement.text: International congress on Buddhist women's role in the sangha, hosted by H.H. the Dalai Lama.

ordinationMovement.title: First bhikshuni ordination in Bhutan
ordinationMovement.text: At the King's request, Bhutan's Je Khenpo fully ordains 144 women — establishing a bhikshuni sangha within Tibetan Buddhism for the first time in modern history.

ordinationMovement.title: Seoul Declaration
ordinationMovement.text: 18th Sakyadhita conference participants reaffirm commitment to ordination work across all traditions.

ordinationMovement.title: Second Bhutan ordination
ordinationMovement.text: At the culmination of Bhutan's Global Peace Prayer Festival, 265 nuns from ten countries receive full ordination — a profound step toward gender equality in spiritual leadership.
